description_long: This object is a hand axe featuring a robust, slightly curved wooden handle and a solid, dark metal head. The handle is made from a light-colored wood, likely ash or hickory, with a smooth finish and a gentle ergonomic curve for improved grip and control. The axe head is forged from steel, with a broad, wedge-shaped blade on one side and a flat poll on the opposite end, suitable for splitting or chopping tasks. The blade edge is straight and sharp, designed for efficient cutting, while the poll is squared off for striking. The head is securely mounted onto the handle using a traditional wedged fitting, with no visible fasteners or modern attachments. There are no visible markings, engravings, or labels on either the handle or the blade.
description: A hand axe with a curved wooden handle and a broad, dark metal blade, designed for chopping and splitting tasks. The head is securely wedged onto the handle, with a smooth, ergonomic grip.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the curved wooden handle and the broad, flat blade extending perpendicularly.
description_view_1: Side profile highlighting the full length of the handle and the wedge-shaped blade with a straight edge.
description_view_2: End-on view displaying the thickness of the blade and the oval cross-section of the handle.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile showing the handle's curvature and the squared poll of the axe head.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle, smooth finish, ergonomic grip, oval cross-section, made from light-colored wood.
attribute_blade_design: Broad, wedge-shaped steel blade with a straight cutting edge and squared poll; single-edged, no beard or decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged fitting, blade head fitted onto handle with no visible fasteners.
